---
title: "Reflection"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
---

## My Learning Journey {.text-right}

This module gave me a practical understanding of how to build and deploy professional digital projects using modern tools like **Quarto**, **Git**, and **GitHub Pages**. It bridged the gap between academic work and real-world software development practices.

## Hands-On Learning {.text-left}

The hands-on learning approach played a key role in my development. By building a portfolio website from scratch, I learned not just the tools, but how to think like a developer — planning structure, writing clean code, and iterating based on feedback.

::: {.callout-tip}
## Key Insight
Building something real is far more valuable than reading about it. Every bug I fixed taught me more than any lecture could.
:::

## Guest Lectures & Inspiration {.text-right}

The guest lectures were another valuable part of this experience. Hearing from professionals who use data analytics and web technologies daily gave me a sense of what the industry expects — and motivated me to go beyond the minimum requirements.

## Module Overview Video {.text-left}

Here is a short overview of what Quarto is and how it enables reproducible publishing:

{{< video https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=_f3latmOhew width="100%" height="400" >}}

::: {.callout-note}
## Why Quarto?
Quarto allows seamless integration of Python code, markdown text, and HTML output — making it perfect for a data analytics portfolio.
:::

## Skills I Developed {.text-right}

| Skill | Tool Used | Proficiency Gained |
|---|---|---|
| Version Control | Git & GitHub | Committing, branching, pushing |
| Web Publishing | GitHub Pages | Live deployment from docs/ |
| Reproducible Reports | Quarto | .qmd files with Python chunks |
| Styling | SCSS / Bootswatch | Custom themes and variables |
| Data Visualisation | Python / Plotly | Interactive charts |

## Challenges I Overcame {.text-left}

> "The only way to learn is to do." — The experience of troubleshooting deployment issues taught me more about web hosting than any tutorial.

- Setting up the correct `output-dir: docs` in `_quarto.yml`
- Writing descriptive Git commit messages consistently
- Ensuring Python code chunks executed correctly in each project page
- Balancing academic workload with portfolio development

## Future Goals {.text-right}

This module has strengthened my ability to present analytical work professionally. Going forward, I plan to:

- [ ] Deploy more advanced data science projects
- [ ] Contribute to open-source repositories
- [ ] Build interactive dashboards with Plotly and Dash
- [ ] Pursue certifications in cloud platforms (Azure / AWS)
- [ ] Apply Business Analytics skills in a professional setting in Ireland

## Final Thoughts {.text-left}

This ePortfolio represents more than just an assignment — it is a **living document** of my skills, growth, and ambitions as a Business Analytics professional. I am proud of what I have built and excited to continue evolving it as I grow in my career.

---
